characters.name: An Ideal Date
characters.description: An Ideal DateGenderMaleSpeciesDog (Greyhound)Relationships• Swanky (Ex-Love Interest)• Countess Von Suave (Owner)ActorN/AStatusAlive An Ideal Dateis a male greyhound who is a race dog at theGreyhound Track, owned byCountess Von Suave, and was the love interest ofSwanky. He only appeared once in "Wish You Were Shear" and is currently unknown what happened to him after Swanky left him forSheep. An Ideal Date is a greyhound breed, having blue-ish gray fur, lime eyes, dark red nose, and is quite thin. He would also wear a shirt that has a large red "1" on the sides of it.

characters.name: Ben Plotz
characters.description: Ben PlotzGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Stu (Friend)ActorKen SchatzStatusAlive  Ben Plotzis the narrator for Sheep in the Big City. Ben is notorious for his outbursts concerning incoherent narratives. Unrealism, cliché, last-minute budgetary changes, nonsensical tropes, and out-of-character writing annoy him to oblivion. While very critical of the scripts he’s given, even cynical at times, Ben Plotz is a theatrical, bubbly personality who puts a lot of effort and passion into his narration job.
characters.personality_section: While very critical of the scripts he’s given, even cynical at times, Ben Plotz is a theatrical, bubbly personality who puts a lot of effort and passion into his narration job.

characters.name: Betsy
characters.description: BetsyGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsN/AActorStephanie D'AbruzzoStatusAlive  Betsyis a ditzy woman who appears mostly withHankon the news and proclaim everything as a "unrelated story" despite it being sometimes related to the main story being told; though she sometimes appears on other shows like"The-You're-Too-Lazy-To-Get-Out-Of-Your-House-And-Go-Shopping Station"for example.

characters.name: Buddy Somebody
characters.description: Buddy SomebodyGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsThe Sandwich (Opponent)ActorN/AStatusAlive Buddy Somebody is a mostly polite and humble man who constantly claims to not care aboutThe Sandwichwhile informing everyone that he is better than him because he is not a ham sandwich, as well as call out his bias at one point during one ofGamey McGameGame’s trivia games. Buddy Somebody has a mostly semi-professional appearance. He has brown skin, wears a pink shirt along with a yellow tie, and has dark grey hair behind his head as well as very little of it on the top of his head. He also has two teal eyes, with one of them being smaller than the other.

characters.name: Completely Powerful Guy
characters.description: Completely Powerful GuyGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsWonderful Boy (Sidekick), Count D'Ten (Villain)ActorKen SchatzStatusAlive  TheCompletely Powerful Guyis a male “superhero” who proclaims to be completely powerful, but tries to avoid to be in any fights with other villains, likeCount D'Tenfor example. He is mostly with his sidekick,Wonderful Boy, who gets annoyed by his refusal to fight back or do anything heroic.

characters.name: Countess Von Suave
characters.description: Countess Von SuaveGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsLady Virginia Richington (Acquaintance)An Ideal Date (Pet)ActorN/AStatusAlive Countess Von Suave is a rich prosperous woman who isLady Virginia Richington's acquaintance and the owner ofAn Ideal Date, a greyhound who was notable for being Swanky's ex-love interest. She makes her first and last appearance in "Wish You Were Shear." Not much is known about Countess Von Suave, as the only things known about her past was that she knew a rich family whom's ancestors came to the United States on the Mayflower.

characters.name: Dr. Oh No No No
characters.description: Dr. Oh No No NoGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• General Specific (Former Associate)ActorN/AStatusUnknown Dr. Oh No No Nois a cliched villainous doctor who works forOFN MB PRAWD (Organization For Nastiness Meanest Badness Pawns Rudeness And World Destruction),a voluntary group who fools other villainous characters (like General Specific) into taking control of their high-tech assets and then entrapping them. He makes his only appearance in “The Wool Is Not Enough.”

characters.name: Farmer John
characters.description: Farmer JohnGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Sheep (Ex-Owned)• General Specific (Enemy)• Private Public (Enemy)ActorJames GodwinStatusAlive Farmer Johnis a mostly self-centered, overbearing and pestering farmer who is notable for beingSheep's ex-owner. His main goal throughout the show is try and catch Sheep, likeGeneral Specific. However, unlike General Specific (who wants Sheep for a sheep-powered ray-gun), Farmer John wants Sheep back at the Farm for “emotional reasons” (as expressed in the pilot: “In the Baa-ginning”) ever since he left for the Big City. Farmer John has tan skin and wears a yellow shirt, blue overalls and brown shoes, along with a red cap. He has a bulbous nose and small seafoam eyes.

characters.name: General Lee Outrageous
characters.description: General Lee OutrageousGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Private Party (Hypeman)• General Specific (First Cousin and Enemy)• Sheep (Possible one-sided friend)ActorJoey MazzarinoStatusAlive  General Lee Outrageous is a stereotypical 1970s disco partier and General Specific's "crazy cousin." General Lee Outrageous is an outrageous personality who thrives on abundant stimuli. Compared to his hypeman Private Party, he is far less cunning and more antagonizing, harassing those who don't submit to his demands to make merry.  When badgering proves unsuccessful, he orders "The Booty Squad" to dance the unsuspecting victim weak.

characters.name: General Public
characters.description: General PublicGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Private Public (Son)ActorJerry NelsonStatusAlive  General Publicis the father ofPrivate Public. He makes his first and last appearence in "Daddy Shearest." General Public has a very high standard and would complain about anything that he finds nonsensical or not to standard. His love for his son, Private Public, is very little as he would often shame him for his career choices and doesn't respect his wishes and rights.

characters.name: General Specific
characters.description: General SpecificGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Private Public (Right-hand man and presumed friend)• Sheep (Enemy)• General Lee Outrageous (Cousin and Enemy)• Officer Specific (Grandfather)• X-Agent (Enemy and Former Friend)ActorKevin Seal, Peter Zhelder (Denmark)StatusAlive  General Specificis the main antagonist ofSheep in the Big City. He isSheep's arch-nemesis,Private Public's boss, and the leader of theSecret Military Organization. He means to capture Sheep, so he can use him to power hisray gunand dominate the world. General Specific is typically defined by his undying determination to capture Sheep. His determination manifests in various ways: mostly in that of over-the-top schemes and poorly-executed attacks. On occasion, he can seem overly prideful and incompetent, but numerous episodes have demonstrated his true intelligence.

characters.name: General Specific’s “Family”
characters.description: Reason:This page is subject for deletion. To help decide if it should be deleted, please enter thoughts in the comment section. General Specific’s “family” consists of a wife and two unnamed kids.  This “family” of his is exclusively featured inWool of the People, alluding to the “family man”  image many politicians employ to acquire the public’s favor. As the skit progresses, it becomes painfully clear this “family” is but a paid actor and two cardboard cutouts. Gag Transcript:

characters.name: German Shepherd
characters.description: TheGerman Shepherdis a minor character who lives inthe Big City. He appears to be hostile towardsSheep. He appears several times in the series, usually for a gag. He has a long nose and a bushy mustache, and wears lederhosen and normally holds a shepherd's cane. German ShepherdGenderMaleSpeciesHumanActorKevin Seal

characters.name: Great Scott
characters.description: Great ScottGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsN/AActorKen SchatzStatusAlive  Great Scottis a cheerful Scotsman who frequently appears and asking "EEE-Yeeeeeeeeesssss?" when someone says the exclamation (or his name), Great Scott. He makes his first appearance in "To Bleat or Not to Bleat".

characters.name: Hank
characters.description: HankGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsN/AActorKen SchatzStatusAlive  Hank is a wrathful male news reporter who often appears alongsideBetsy, on the news and reports on events in the Big City. He often goes straight to the point and promotes opinions that are mostly popular in the Big City.

characters.name: Hank

characters.name: Jay
characters.description: JayGenderMaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsN/AActorKen SchatzStatusAlive  Jayis an optimistic man who is the first to read out signs, after a character sees one, before expressing his thoughts that are either about the sign or why he likes reading them.

characters.name: Lady Virginia Richington
characters.actor_name: Ruth Buzzi (Pilot Only), Stephanie D'Abruzzo (T.V. series)
characters.description: Lady Virginia RichingtonGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Swanky (Pet)• Sheep (Enemy)• Countess Von Suave (Acquaintance)ActorRuth Buzzi (Pilot Only), Stephanie D'Abruzzo (T.V. series)StatusAlive  Lady Virginia Richington, or also known asLady Richington, is a snobby and selfish rich woman who is notable for being the owner ofSwankyand having a hatred of sheep, especiallySheep. She made her first appearance in "In The Baa-ginning" but under a different design, before her design changed in her next appearance in "Be Still My Bleating Heart" and remained that way for the rest of the show.

characters.name: Lisa Rental
characters.description: Lisa RentalGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanRelationships• Sheep (One-sided friend)• X-Agent (Afraid of)ActorStephanie D'AbruzzoStatusAlive Lisa Rental is a young small girl who is mostly seen throughout the show trying to have and keepSheepas a pet because she had mistaken him for a dog. Her attitude and voice can be obnoxious to some. Lisa loves to refer to Sheep as a"doggy woggy poggy noggy soggy floggy moggy shroggy loggy doggy."She meets General Specific she convinces him that Sheep was a sheep dog by having sheep obey her commands (not knowing her true colors at the time, Sheep played along). This was only one account that Sheep was actually happy with Lisa.

characters.name: Little Bo Peep
characters.description: Little Bo PeepGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsN/AActorN/AStatusUnknown  Little Bo Peep is a young girl who, according to her, is the “cutest girl” in Happy Land and has lost her own sheep. She would try to join with others to get them help find her lost sheep, only to then get literarily thrown away.

characters.name: Officer Specific
characters.description: Reason:This page is so indeed broken. I'm going to fix it sometime in the future. - ThatAnonymousUser8518 Officer Specific was the Grandfather to General Specific and General Lee Outrageous. Officer Specific was the head of the police force in the big city.  He started chasing sheep's Grandfather when he accidentally ate his sandwich.  He and the entire police force went to hunt down Sheep but, missed with every effort made.  While chasing sheep he met Madame Richington whom he show his gentleman qualities to her that seem to woo her.  Later own it shown that Madame Richington decided to help Officer Specific in capturing sheep in which he did.

characters.name: Oxymoron
characters.description: OxymoronGenderMaleSpeciesOxRelationshipsN/AActorN/AStatusAlive  Oxymoronis an ox who is the main mascot for Oxymoron, not to be confused in the business. He appears at the end of most Oxymoron's commericals and makes a roaring sound after their slogan, "From Oxymoron", is said; that doesn't always happen though.
